answer: Enable IA under UEM Console -> Groups & Settings -> All Settings -> Apps -> Settings and Policies -> Security Policies -> Integrated Authentication -> Add allowed URLs.
Configure certificate-based IA: Upload CA cert to UEM -> Create SDK Profile with IA enabled -> Add certificate credential payload -> Assign to app. Test by accessing the allowed URL.
UEM supports 3 enrolment modes: 1) MDM (Mobile Device Management) Mode - full device management. 2) Standalone Mode - app-level management without MDM. 3) Register Mode - lightweight registration.
Go to UEM Console -> Groups & Settings -> All Settings -> Devices & Users -> General -> Enrollment -> Restrictions -> Enable User Unenrollment option.
Enable Shared Device mode in UEM Console -> Groups & Settings -> All Settings -> Devices & Users -> General -> Shared Device. Then test login/logout with different users on the same device.
Configure OCSP under UEM Console -> Groups & Settings -> All Settings -> System -> Enterprise Integration -> OCSP -> Add responder URL. Test with certificates that have OCSP responder configured.
Go to UEM Console -> Devices -> Profiles & Resources -> Profiles -> Add -> Add Profile -> Select platform (iOS) -> Configure payload settings -> Save & Publish.
Go to UEM Console -> Apps & Books -> Applications -> Select App -> More -> Revoke Token. Then test 403 response and silent HMAC refresh flow in the SDK app.

SDK Using two apis to check device and application enrolment and other compliance status, Application Status API (/appstatus):Used to fetch the status of a specific application (e.g., version, compliance, group membership) on a device. The SDK uses this to check if the app is up-to-date, properly enrolled, or meets organisational requirements. Used to fetch the overall status of the device, including compliance, enrollment, and other device-level attributes. The SDK uses this to determine if the device itself is compliant and allowed to access corporate resources.When the user moves the application from background to foreground, the WS1-iOS SDK starts and checks the device status. If the device status changes from enrolled to unenrolled or unknown, it updates the enrollment details. Based on this update, the SDK and Hub-iOS trigger the application and user data wipe call.

A basic query from Hub-iOS considers the use case where Hub-iOS enrolls with a staging account and the user logs out of the staging account. The SDK still can send logs
I have discussed with SDK team and tested How SDK Logs work As of the 26.02.00 release, the SDK priorities HMAC authentication when available. If the check-in state is staging logout and HMAC is unavailable, the SDK sends logs using OneTimeLogin. This login remains valid for five minutes from the first API request with the OneTimeLogToken. Once the token expires, the SDK marks it as used and does refresh it upon new user login. When HMAC unavailable and OneLogToken become invalid, SDK will try to send logs with Predefined HMAC. Predefined HMAC is become deprecated and unreliable that’s reason SDK introduce to oneTimeLogToken overcome unpredictability of Predefined  HMAC. Note: If SDK initialization fails, the user cannot request logs from UEM. The user can send logs only after successful SDK initialization

Exiting implementation Do not display default contact or email ID. First, check if the Hub service is enabled; if so, retrieve data from it. If the Hub service is disabled, retrieve data from UEM. Show the option only if contact or email is available; otherwise, disable and hide it. Configuration UEM configuration:  Navigate to: Groups & Settings → All Settings → Devices & Users (or Devices) → General → Enrolment → Customisation →Enter support contact and email Hub Service -> Employee Self-Service -> Support -> Contact Information ->Edit Information →  Enter email and Support Phone Number

Admin can publish any app via UEM console example: Amazon Steps to publish App:
Log in to console → Select OG → Resources → Native App → Amazon → Edit → Term of Use → Manage Term of Use → Create new ToC version → Save, assign, and publish Enroll Hub: Enroll Hub → Installed Amazon app from Catalog → Accept ToC before installation → Accept install → Done Publish new ToC again → Select OG → Resources → Native App → Amazon → Edit → Term of Use → Manage Term of Use → Create new ToC version → Save, assign, and publish Quit Hub app → Launch app → Popup Term of Use and ToC banner appear
